docs.title: Sorsa Ingestion Documentation
docs.text:
This documentation set covers the newly implemented ingestion app under Sorsa/app.

The implementation is:

  • Cockroach-first
  • strict upsert-based
  • checkpoint-aware
  • local runnable now, worker-portable later

docs.title: What is implemented
docs.text:
• API client support for:
  • /search-tweets
  • /comments
  • /user-tweets
  • /score-id/{x-id}
  • 72-hour ingestion window for keyword search with time-sliced parallel fetch.
  • Per-key rate limiting and retry handling for 429/5xx/network errors.
  • Unified run tracking and checkpoint persistence in CockroachDB.
  • Post-level deduplication by post_id with project_keywords merge semantics.

docs.title: Code layout
docs.text:
• app/config.py
  • app/clients/sorsa_client.py
  • app/db/connection.py
  • app/db/repository.py
  • app/pipeline/search_ingestor.py
  • app/pipeline/aux_ingestors.py
  • app/pipeline/orchestrator.py
  • app/cli.py
  • main.py

docs.title: DDL files
docs.text:
Original DDL (unchanged):

  • Mindshare DDL/ddl mindshare.sql

New ingestion-focused DDL copy:

  • ddl/ddl_mindshare_ingestion.sql

docs.title: High-level flow
docs.text:
flowchart TD
    localCli[LocalCLIOrWorkerEntry] --> orchestrator[IngestionOrchestrator]
    orchestrator --> search[SearchIngestor72hSlices]
    search --> sorsaSearch[SorsaPOSTsearch-tweets]
    search --> repo[IngestionRepository]
    repo --> cockroach[CockroachDB]

    orchestrator --> aux[AuxiliaryIngestors]
    aux --> comments[SorsaPOSTcomments]
    aux --> userTweets[SorsaPOSTuser-tweets]
    aux --> score[SorsaGETscore-id]
    aux --> repo

docs.title: app/config.py
docs.text:
• Loads all runtime settings via environment variables.
  • Parses multiple API keys from a comma-separated input.

docs.title: app/clients/sorsa_client.py
docs.text:
• Implements endpoint wrappers:
  • search_tweets
  • comments
  • user_tweets
  • score_id
  • Enforces per-key rate control.
  • Retries transient failures (429, 5xx, network timeout).

docs.title: app/db/repository.py
docs.text:
• Persists ingestion run lifecycle.
  • Persists raw API payloads.
  • Performs strict upsert into mindshare_post.
  • Maintains endpoint/window checkpoints.

docs.title: app/pipeline/search_ingestor.py
docs.text:
• Splits the last 72 hours into slices.
  • Executes search ingestion concurrently by slice.
  • Tracks cursor progress in checkpoints.

docs.title: app/pipeline/aux_ingestors.py
docs.text:
• Ingests comments for discovered posts.
  • Ingests user timelines for discovered users.
  • Fetches and upserts user scores.

docs.title: app/pipeline/orchestrator.py
docs.text:
• Creates run record.
  • Runs search phase, then aux phases.
  • Marks run completed/failed.

docs.title: Worker portability model
docs.text:
The local runner and future worker both call the same orchestrator API:

  • local: python main.py --project-keyword ...
  • worker: invoke IngestionOrchestrator.run_project_ingestion(...) directly

No ingestion logic is tied to local-only assumptions.

docs.title: Data Model
docs.text: This page documents ingestion-specific schema behavior in ddl/ddl_mindshare_ingestion.sql.

docs.title: Design principles
docs.text:
• Strict upsert-only writes.
  • One logical tweet row per post_id.
  • No duplicate tweet rows across projects.
  • project_keywords is merged as set-like array on conflict.

docs.title: mindshare.mindshare_post
docs.text:
Purpose:

  • normalized tweet storage with one-row-per-post_id.

Important fields:

  • post_id (PK)
  • user_x_id
  • project_keywords (TEXT[])
  • engagement counts
  • relationship ids (retweeted_post_id, replied_post_id, etc.)
  • post_created_at
  • ingestion metadata (last_ingested_run_id, last_seen_at)

docs.title: raw_data.raw_post_ingestion
docs.text:
Purpose:

  • raw endpoint payload storage for replay/debugging.

Uniqueness:

  • primary key (post_id, endpoint)

docs.title: mindshare.ingestion_run
docs.text:
Purpose:

  • one row per ingestion trigger with status and timing.

docs.title: mindshare.ingestion_window_checkpoint
docs.text:
Purpose:

  • checkpoint cursor/state for endpoint windows and slices.

docs.title: Upsert conflict behavior
docs.text:
For mindshare_post on post_id conflict:

  • merge project_keywords with existing keywords
  • update engagement counts from latest payload
  • keep non-empty full_text
  • refresh last_seen_at and last_ingested_run_id

docs.title: Merge semantics diagram
docs.text:
flowchart LR
    incoming[IncomingTweetpost_id123keywordB] --> conflict{post_idExists}
    conflict -->|No| insert[InsertNewRowproject_keywordsB]
    conflict -->|Yes| merge[MergeKeywordsAndUpdateMutableFields]
    merge --> result[SingleRowpost_id123keywordsAplusB]

docs.title: 1) Search phase (/search-tweets)
docs.text:
• Compute until = now_utc and since = now_utc - 72h.
  • Split time range into configured slices.
  • Assign slices to API-key aliases.
  • For each slice:
  • fetch pages using next_cursor
  • persist raw rows
  • upsert normalized rows
  • update checkpoint status (running -> completed)

docs.title: 2) Comments phase (/comments)
docs.text:
• Iterate discovered post IDs from search phase.
  • Fetch and ingest paginated comments.
  • Write endpoint checkpoint per post window.

docs.title: 3) User timeline phase (/user-tweets)
docs.text:
• Iterate discovered user IDs from search phase.
  • Fetch and ingest paginated user timeline tweets.
  • Write endpoint checkpoint per user window.

docs.title: 4) Score phase (/score-id/{x-id})
docs.text:
• Iterate discovered user IDs.
  • Fetch score payload and upsert mindshare_user.
  • Write endpoint checkpoint per user.

docs.title: Retry and rate-limit strategy
docs.text:
Applied in SorsaClient:

  • per-key RPS limiter
  • retries for:
  • 429
  • 5xx
  • network/timeout
  • fail-fast for non-retryable client errors after response handling

docs.title: Sequence
docs.text:
sequenceDiagram
    participant Trigger as Runner
    participant Orch as Orchestrator
    participant Search as SearchIngestor
    participant Aux as AuxiliaryIngestors
    participant Repo as Repository
    participant DB as CockroachDB
    participant API as SorsaAPI

    Trigger->>Orch: run_project_ingestion(keyword)
    Orch->>Repo: create_run()
    Repo->>DB: INSERT ingestion_run

    Orch->>Search: ingest_72h()
    Search->>API: POST /search-tweets (paged)
    Search->>Repo: upsert raw + normalized + checkpoint
    Repo->>DB: UPSERT

    Orch->>Aux: ingest_comments_for_posts()
    Aux->>API: POST /comments (paged)
    Aux->>Repo: upsert raw + normalized + checkpoint

    Orch->>Aux: ingest_user_tweets()
    Aux->>API: POST /user-tweets (paged)
    Aux->>Repo: upsert raw + normalized + checkpoint

    Orch->>Aux: ingest_scores()
    Aux->>API: GET /score-id/{x-id}
    Aux->>Repo: upsert user + checkpoint

    Orch->>Repo: mark_run_finished(completed)
    Repo->>DB: UPDATE ingestion_run

docs.title: Prerequisites
docs.text:
• Python 3.12+
  • CockroachDB instance reachable through postgresql+asyncpg SQLAlchemy URL
  • One or more Sorsa API keys

docs.title: Environment variables
docs.text:
Required:

  • COCKROACH_DATABASE_URL
  • SORSA_API_KEYS (comma separated)

Optional tuning:

  • SORSA_BASE_URL (default: https://api.sorsa.io/v3)
  • SORSA_PER_KEY_RPS (default: 20)
  • SEARCH_SLICE_COUNT (default: 20)
  • SEARCH_ORDER (default: latest)
  • SORSA_MAX_RETRIES (default: 4)
  • SORSA_RETRY_429_SLEEP_SECONDS (default: 1.0)
  • SORSA_RETRY_5XX_SLEEP_SECONDS (default: 2.0)

docs.title: Install dependencies
docs.text:
cd Sorsa
pip install -e .

docs.title: Apply ingestion DDL
docs.text:
Use:

  • ddl/ddl_mindshare_ingestion.sql

Do not edit/replace:

  • Mindshare DDL/ddl mindshare.sql

docs.title: Run locally
docs.text:
python main.py --project-keyword <keyword>

Example:

python main.py --project-keyword Acurast

docs.title: Build docs site
docs.text:
From Sorsa/ingestion-docs:

mkdocs serve

or

mkdocs build

docs.title: Common runtime checks
docs.text:
1. Confirm run record exists in mindshare.ingestion_run.
  2. Inspect checkpoint table for stalled/failed windows.
  3. Check recent rows in raw_data.raw_post_ingestion.
  4. Verify merged keyword behavior in mindshare.mindshare_post.

docs.title: Last runs
docs.text:
SELECT run_id, project_keyword, run_status, started_at, finished_at
FROM mindshare.ingestion_run
ORDER BY started_at DESC
LIMIT 20;

docs.title: Failed windows
docs.text:
SELECT run_id, endpoint, window_id, status, error_message, updated_at
FROM mindshare.ingestion_window_checkpoint
WHERE status = 'failed'
ORDER BY updated_at DESC
LIMIT 50;

docs.title: Verify keyword merge on duplicate post
docs.text:
SELECT post_id, project_keywords, last_seen_at
FROM mindshare.mindshare_post
WHERE post_id = <tweet_id>;

docs.title: Tuning guidance
docs.text:
• Increase throughput:
  • add more API keys to SORSA_API_KEYS
  • increase SEARCH_SLICE_COUNT cautiously
  • Reduce rate-limit pressure:
  • lower SORSA_PER_KEY_RPS
  • increase retry sleep values

docs.title: Known limitations in current implementation
docs.text:
• Resume-from-checkpoint reader is not yet fully wired (checkpoints are written, but restart flow currently begins a fresh run).
  • Auxiliary endpoint phases currently run through a single selected key alias.
  • Batch write optimization can be improved for higher-volume ingest.

These are next recommended hardening steps.
